item.title: Japan’s bonds
item.description: Plus, India’s wobble

item.description: Investors should pay attention to the struggles of the consumer packaged goods industry

item.title: How investors need to adapt to a new era of ‘spikeflation’
item.description: Diversification is the first defence against uncertainty and investors should seek inflation hedges like commodities

item.title: Javier Milei: Argentina invites AI to free itself
item.description: As we enter a new era of technology, AI must be permitted to develop without premature regulation

item.title: Big Four stronger than ever as UK audit clampdown eases
item.description: The firms have emerged with their dominance entrenched and their businesses enhanced

item.title: Nigella Lawson: How to rescue sad summer crops
item.description: When strawberry season yields disappointment, here are a handful of strategies to perk up dull produce

item.title: Japanese hardware stocks are having a moment — again
item.description: The current surge plays to the country’s industrial strengths

item.title: Europe’s tightrope walk on tech sovereignty
item.description: EU should be wary of curbing access to US providers as it tries to nurture its own

item.title: Barclays hopes to boost UK economy with enlightened self-interest
item.description: Bank wants the government to make changes that encourage more foreign companies to manage their cash from the UK

item.title: Warsh needs to separate his roles at the Fed
item.description: This is how the new chair should approach his first rate-setting meeting

item.title: The home climate fixes that can survive the populist backlash
item.description: Some measures are gathering pace because they aim to save money as well as the planet

item.title: Electoral reform for the UK? Don’t bet on it
item.description: Fractured party politics and enthusiasm from Labour’s hopefuls are not a sign of imminent proportional voting

item.title: Turkey’s frozen frontier with Armenia starts to thaw
item.description: An outbreak of peacemaking that should rank as a minor miracle in a troubled world

item.title: Hyperscalers and the equity tap: more to come
item.description: Plus, corporate culture and returns

item.title: Ebola outbreak stirs familiar grim sense of foreboding
item.description: The Bundibugyo strain is a reminder that serious health threats can escalate from under-the-radar pathogens

item.title: Swedish business bristles over EU interventions
item.description: Lawyers and financiers complain that policymakers could be undermining the success of the country’s model

item.title: The UK is stuck in a rollercoaster queue
item.description: British theme parks are a fitting symbol of the country’s supply-side economic woes

item.title: A guide for the perplexed on AI
item.description: Should we think of this technology as boon, bane or bubble?

item.title: The AI boom gives 1990s IT brands a second crack at youth
item.description: Top-line growth is exciting Wall Street even though the groups are just one link in a much larger AI-related domino rally

item.title: How can we persuade our daughter to have a prenup?
item.description: She is getting married this summer but stands to inherit a large amount of money from us

item.title: Index investing will evolve with mega IPOs
item.description: New offerings will be a test but markets ultimately absorb new supply and determine prices over time

item.title: Why ultra-luxury cruises are at risk of running aground
item.description: The core principles of luxury can lose their efficacy on the high seas

item.title: Cuba’s unavoidable reckoning
item.description: US pressure is of dubious morality, but Havana has little choice but to do a deal

item.title: The China-US tech truce is fragile
item.description: A new wave of supply chain conflict is brewing

item.title: Wise money laundering probe hits at fintech’s fundamental flaw
item.description: Investigation comes at a particularly bad time given its recent transatlantic stock market move
